title: stapelia pulchra
intro: Stapelia pulchra, also known as the "Poison Bottle" or "Queen of the Night," is a succulent plant species native to the deserts of Southern Africa. The plant's unique appearance is due to its striking, star-shaped red flowers, which are often mistaken for a poisonous substance, hence its name. Traditionally, the Stapelia pulchra plant has been used in folk medicine for its purported medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, and it is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's tuberous roots and stems are often dried and used in infusions, tinctures, and ointments to harness its medicinal properties.
